articles.title: Storm, rain alert in 26 Rajasthan districts: Wet spell till July 1; Jaisalmer, Barmer sizzle at 43°C - Bhaskar English
articles.full_text:
Storm, rain alert in 26 Rajasthan districts:Wet spell till July 1; Jaisalmer, Barmer sizzle at 43°C
- Copy link
An alert for thunderstorms and rain has been issued for 26 districts of Rajasthan on Sunday. According to the Meteorological Department, the current weather activity is likely to continue until July 1.
On Saturday afternoon, the weather changed across parts of the Udaipur and Kota divisions. Clouds gathered over several districts, bringing light to moderate rainfall in parts of Udaipur, Pratapgarh, Dungarpur, Banswara, Chittorgarh, Rajsamand, Pali, Sirohi, Jhalawar and Baran.
Banswara recorded the highest rainfall, while Bikaner and Jaisalmer remained among the hottest places in the state. Several areas received between 1 and 3 inches of rain. The Bakal River in Udaipur district also started flowing after the showers.
Western Rajasthan continues to reel under heat
While southern districts received rain, most parts of the Bharatpur, Jaipur, Ajmer, Bikaner and Jodhpur divisions remained dry on Saturday. Intense sunshine pushed temperatures above 43°C in western Rajasthan.
Phalodi recorded the state's highest maximum temperature at 43.8°C, followed by Bikaner (43.2°C), Jaisalmer (43°C), Barmer (42.9°C), Sri Ganganagar (42.8°C) and Churu (42.2°C).
Other cities also recorded high temperatures, including Jodhpur (41.8°C), Tonk (41.6°C), Fatehpur (41.5°C), Dausa (41.5°C), Pilani (40.7°C), Pali (40.5°C), Alwar (40.4°C), Kota (40.2°C) and Karauli (40.2°C).
Highest rainfall recorded in Banswara
During the past 24 hours, Danpur in Banswara received 72 mm of rain. Other major rainfall figures include:
Heavy rain damages crops in Churu
Strong winds and heavy rain that lashed Ghanghu village in Churu district late on Friday caused extensive damage to standing crops. Local farmers reported significant losses.
Weather update from major cities
Jaipur
Despite a yellow alert for thunderstorms and rain, the city remained hot and humid throughout Saturday. The sky stayed mostly clear, with bright sunshine during the day. The maximum temperature was 39.8°C, while the minimum was 29.4°C.
Jodhpur
Clouds appeared during the afternoon, and cool winds started blowing around 5 pm. The maximum temperature settled at 39°C. Rain has been forecast for three of the next seven days.
Udaipur
Clouds gathered by afternoon, bringing rain to several rural areas. Vallabhnagar witnessed heavy rainfall, while Gogunda also received showers. The maximum temperature was 37°C and the minimum 29.5°C. The Bakal River started flowing after the rainfall.
Ajmer
The city remained hot under strong sunshine. The maximum temperature reached 38.9°C, while the minimum was 26°C. Humidity added to the discomfort.
Alwar
The weather remained hot and humid despite a forecast of thunderstorms and rain. Temperatures hovered around 39°C during the afternoon, but no rainfall was recorded.
Sikar
Strong sunshine continued throughout the day, with occasional cloud cover. The maximum temperature crossed the 40°C mark after nearly two weeks, reaching 41.5°C at the Fatehpur weather station. The minimum temperature was 26.2°C.
Kota
The city experienced hot and humid conditions, with the maximum temperature at 40.2°C and the minimum at 29.4°C. The Meteorological Department has issued a yellow alert for thunderstorms and rain in Kota and nearby districts for the next three days.
